The '''imperfect''' (or '''imperfective''') in Aelaki refers to ongoing, incomplete action. It is realized through the [[Continuous|continuative aspect]], which uses C₂ reduplication.

== Formation ==

The imperfective is expressed through the continuative aspect, formed by reduplicating the second root consonant (C₂).

Using the root ''zh-r-n'' "live" (base form: ''zharan''):

{| class="wikitable"
! Aspect !! Form !! Pattern !! Meaning
|-
| Base || zharan || C₁V₁C₂V₂C₃ || (someone) lives
|-
| Continuative (imperfective) || zhororon || C₂ reduplication || living (in progress)
|}

== Behavior across tenses ==

Unlike the [[Perfect|perfect]] aspects, the continuative does not change its function between past and present tense. It consistently expresses an action in progress.

== Contrast with perfect ==

{| class="wikitable"
! Category !! Aspect !! Example !! Meaning
|-
| '''Imperfective''' || [[Continuous|Continuative]] || zhororon || living (in progress)
|-
| Perfective (atelic) || [[Atelic|Atelic perfect]] || zhoron || is alive (state achieved)
|-
| Perfective (telic) || [[Telic|Telic perfect]] || zheren || lived (life completed)
|}

== See also ==

* [[Continuous]]
* [[Perfect]]
* [[Habitual]]

[[Category:Continuities?]]
[[Category:git synced pages]]
